analysis.name: Lori Astheimer Best
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 158 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Honestly just a really great professor. Very clear and informative lectures. Its clear she cares about her students doing well. It is a very content heavy class with very difficult concepts. Also exam heavy, 5 midterms, optional final. Although this class was hard Dr. A had lots of extra credit, and lots of help opportunities.

there are not a lot of assignments to help you if you dont do well on the 5 exams we have. she talks so fast, the only plus is that you can do the class on zoom. The exams were kinda hard and i would not recommending taking this if you are a normal psych student.

Truly the best professor I've ever been blessed to have. PLEASE attend the most lectures you can because they are the most comprehensive and engaging hours of my college experience so far. She has so much extra credit that it is hard to not get an A in this class. Attendance is through TopHat and you can join from zoom if needed, but go in person!!

She is amazing. Dr. A offers a zoom link to watch class live while not attending in person. Quizzes are take home and multiple choice. She is very passionate about neuroscience. The class is very big but she does encourage participation and talking with people near you. She is amazing, even if science is not your thing you can do great.

Dr. A explains the material clearly and makes sure to clarify anything people ask about. She encourages questions and answers in easy to understand ways. Assignments: weekly 15 min or less surveys and one final project.

analysis.name: Alexandra Jesse
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 53 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. unless this class is needed for your major I wouldn't take it. 80% of the final grade is exams (4 exams, 1 final), she records lectures but you can't access it unless you have an excused absence (like being sick), and she talks really soft so you can't hear her well. she's sweet as a person but not the greatest professorl

Avoid this prof is possible! Writes her own exams (1st language isn't english), so some questions are confusing. Attendance is mandatory, only get 2 absences. After 1st exam every ~5th class is another exam. Doesn't show final grade on canvas, so no idea how screwed I am. Nice person but not good prof. Avoid, avoid, avoid!!!!

Really boring class and lectures are really dry. You have to pay very good attention in class and when reading the book if you want to do well. Crutches are there to help (drop the lowest test grade.. etc) but still tests and in class writing assignments are really tough. Definitely need to drop the lowest grades and do all SONA extra credit.

Has 4 exams, including final, drops one of three lowest exams all graded 20% of your grade. If you honestly do well in the first 3 you dont have to worry too much on the final since they are all graded the same. Loved her little accent especially when she says PLUE instead of BLUE. You will do fine if you are passionate about the subject.

I loved Professor Jesse's class. If you go to class and take notes the exams are very easy. Most of the people I'd heard in class complaining that she was difficult to hear are the same people who would spend all of class talking or on their phones. She also gives study guides that are very good exam prep. Over all good teacher and I learned a lot

analysis.name: Craig Albertson
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Honestly disagree with his ratings. He is a nice guy, content is hard but it's a 582 level class what do u expect. He provided study guides that were nearly identical to exam so it's 100% manageable despite being open response if you just study that. I find the reading quizzes to be harder than exams. Lab fairly easy. Great lecturer imo
He actually teaches like a normal person idk how to explain it but he swears so much and is so real in the best way possible. Hard class but the way he teaches it is great

This class was a lot more challenging this expected. There was so much information to memorize and just the overall content was not what I was looking forward to. Exams were hard overall and required a lot of studying. Some cool topics and really nice professor maybe just was not for me.
Just don't take any of his classes, does not know how to communicate and his exams and grading are just ridiculous, wouldn't recommend
Same guy here. Also took a picture with him and his fish tanks. 10/10 experience.

analysis.name: Andrew Havens
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 108 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: If your shaky on calc or math in general Havens will not help you. Left each class not really learning too much and going to the SI sessions became part of my schedule. He just reads off the slides and expects you to absorb the information and his explanations were no help. Considered not going to classes many times--> Went anyways--> Regretted it.

Epitome of evil.average grade on both exams were in the 59-65 range, and instead of offering help or extra credit for struggling students, his advice for them was to drop the class, it's pretty apparent he takes pride in students struggling. If you have taken calc before, i'd advise you to stay out of lecture and learn the material on your own.

Hated this class. He overcomplicated topics that couldve been explained more simply. He is also lowkey rude to students. If you didnt take calc in high school DO NOT TAKE IT WITH HAVENS. Now I have to retake it because I bombed the midterm, and there was no coming back from it. If you didn't hear me clearly the first time, do not take this class.

If you enjoy life to any sort of degree, do not take 127 with this professor. Besides the fact that he explains arbitrarily easy concepts using graduate material, making them far more complicated than need be, he just genuinely sucks at actually making sure his students understand the material. I suggest going to a different professor's lecture.

He often laughed at students for answering incorrectly, creating an uncomfortable environment. While homework and quizzes were conducted online (two per week), the final exams were in person. Don't recommend raising your hand in class because even if you get it right, he will undermine it and make it sound wrong. Overcomplicates everything.

analysis.name: Annie Raymond
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: If you come into uni with no previous calc experience (like I did) youre cooked. Certain concepts just dont get explained and you have to self-teach no matter how many lectures you show up to. She'd teach for the entire 50-minute lecture and I'd walk out feeling like I knew less and less every time. Easy to cheat on hw and quizzes to save ur grade!

The average was low, but I I think she is very intelligent, inspirational, and willing to explain any concept to you. It was a short class and the course chair controlled most everything in terms of grading, material, and deadlines. She taught very fast, so prep before, ask ?'s, and do practice exams!!

Lectures often made simple concepts confusing, and I rarely left class with clarity. Exam averages were low, there is no curve, and handwriting made notes hard to follow. I taught myself most material, making this class tough on your GPA. DO NOT TAKE!

Worst professor Ive had. Did not learn anything from her and had to do everything on my own. You will get caught off guard during exam, and she makes simple things seem extremely complex. Seems nice, but honestly does not outweigh the horrors of this class. There is no curving in this class, and no leniency with missed assignments. DO NOT TAKE!!!

Raymond was a by far the best professor available for 127, however the class was horrendous and she failed to explain the content clearly. You NEED to study- homework, lectures, and exams barely relate. To her benefit, her practice exams are the best across the course and the Course Chair is at fault for most issues.

analysis.name: Soroosh Azizi
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 44 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: People are just mad because a class got to take a test online because canvas was all messed up for an hour. Isenberg honestly just gives kids the mentality that everything is an easy A... this school a joke. Study for your test next time pal instead of moaning and yelling.  Hate isn't fully deserved let the guy not stress because you can't study

He always prepares a study guide with everything you need to know before the exam and videos on how to complete the HWs step by step. Exams are all multiple choice online and the answers are not there to trick you. Very straight forward and to the point. Entertaining in class and cares about his students participating.

Might be the worst professor. Soroosh has no business teaching an online class, or teaching a course in general. He should consider any other profession regarding finance because he doesn't teach or know how to properly. He has very strict grading policies with few assignments. If you mess up on one exam there is pretty much no coming back from it.

Expects students to perform excel formulas using a financial calculator for In-person exams (1 MINUTE) per question. No time to double check your work even if you finish each question within that minute. And if you don't have a financial calculator, good luck! Double sided cheat sheets are allowed, best of luck fitting 50 pages of exam material in!

Funniest professor I have ever had which keeps the lectures super engaging. Even at 8am he always comes with great energy, and he is clearly passionate about what he teaches. He is strict about due dates and struggled a bit figuring out how to administer exams since it was his first semester @UM. Cant rec Azizi enough, will seek for future courses.

analysis.name: Eric Sarfo Amponsah
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 178 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Completely unable to maintain interest in class or explain things. Sets literally impossible quiz times, enforces them with a rigidity that suggests he mostly loves a power trip. Regularly takes days to respond to emails. Has difficulty answering student questions clearly. Sets unclear standards for grading tests and enforces them rigidly

This professor does not care about people in extenuating situations. I tried to get help from him but he rejected every request from me. All other professors offered help and weight adjustment to give me a chance to pass the course. He is the worst professor I have ever met this semester.
He's quite condescending, and also automatically assumes students just want to cheat and get away with things. That will be his default perception if you ask him about anything.

If you have no external issues, no disabilities, then you're fine taking this prof. If you do, then avoid him. Even if you are registered with disability services/ Dean of Students, he won't work with you. He might not even believe you.
If he's your only option, and you're having trouble, you're better off not approaching him and just try to pass.

You will learn nothing from taking Math235 with Eric. I repeat, NOTHING! He weaponizes exams instead of using them to test understanding, and his poor attitude towards teaching is already palpable from the start. Even if you do well, you'll walk away feeling worn out. This guy does NOT deserve a paycheck as he thinks students are beneath him.

analysis.name: Anita Milman
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
Lots of resources to read up on (often like 10+ pages).
Lectures are a little on the boring side but it's feasible to just be on your phone and look at the slides later.
Great professor :)

Professor Milman made this class interesting and fun. I looked forward to it. Group work, class, activities, and reading quizzes are the important chunk of your grade. I took the class as honors and the projects she had us do made us very passionate about the subject of environmental policy. This was a class every major should be required to take.

I had her for NRC 309. She did a great job at making our lectures not feel like lectures by making us get into discussions with small groups at our tables, sometimes doing mini projects. Homework was mainly just annotating and commenting on Perusall readings; tedious but pretty easy.  The course was somewhat difficult but expected for a 300 level.

Very enthusiastic and passionate about policy. 10 HW quizzes straight from textbook 3 exams (sorta hard especially the multiple choice parts) and class assignments done in table groups. Attendance is mandatory but never actually taken. Overall a pretty easy intro to environmental policy class-PS 1st month is just intro to policy.

this was a good class to take if you are interested in the workings of how policies are made! i loved that we got the option to actually experience "making laws" and used games to help our learning along. easy class; only two exams and most hw were reading assignments

analysis.name: Raphael Arku
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: **this is for pubhlth203** he didn't stay on topic most of the time, would just kind of ramble through while reading directly off of the slides. very rarely did i learn more from going than just reading the slides, but oh well. he's nice so it's hard to dislike him. he's a great researcher who's skills just don't translate to teaching.

***FOR PUBHLTH 203***
Great guy to talk to, genuinely interested in hearing him lecture every time.
Lots of notes to be written.

This class was so confusing. There's no clear grading criteria, and he seems to just come up with stuff for us to do as he goes. Assignments are just busy work, and he assigned a group project with no rubric. Lectures are dull and while he's a nice enough guy, don't waste your time with this class unless you absolutely have to. Textbook used once.

Prof Arku is very nice and the 360 lectures are very clear/interesting/informational. Most of the work is group work so its not that difficult. The class itself is kind of boring and he does sometimes cold call but honestly its not that bad.

HORRIBLE. A lot of small one page papers, a group project, a longer paper and an exam. None of which are explained very well. He just seems to wing it and the class was all over the place. There is only one exam and it is cumulative, and his lectures don't inspire you to come to class so definitely don't take this class with him unless you have to.

analysis.name: Robert Bowse
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 41 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: His feedback is alright but he gives so much unnecessary busy work compared to other professors you can take. I would recommend someone else.

Professor Bowse is a good professor in a very useful class. No exams, just weekly assignments which make up for 80% of your grade. Class is pretty useful in getting you prepared for internships, as you work on resumes/cover letters. Some assignments are longer than others, but nothing too bad. Do the assignments and show up, and you will get an A.

The class has WAYYYY too much work for a 1 credit course. To add insult to injury the work is graded obscenely harshly. If you are able to take this class with anyone else do it. In terms of how he actually is as a professor he's actually not bad, very clear and engaging, just assigns way too much work.

Class is insanely easy. Literally show up, do the homework and online quizzes, and you'll get an A. It's also incredibly helpful for building a great resume and making your LinkedIn professional. It's worth paying attention and putting effort in too, as most of the material is applicable to real life

Professor Bowse has quite a deceptive rating of 4.3. His grading is extremely picky and assignments must be completed HIS way or no way. In terms of workload, this Junior year writing class has considerably more work than other professors.

analysis.name: Kathleen Brown-Perez
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 46 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Kathleen is literally one of the best professors I've ever had. To pass, all you really need to do is show up and participate in the discussions. She even bought our class donuts. I highly recommend her and respect her as a professor.

Great professor with a love for teaching and the course material. She was an excellent instructor to have for my first semester of college. Her course was mostly rooted in group discussion. One of the few instructors for 201H that does not require you to buy any materials. I wish I could have her again.

Kathleen is a great professor for Criminal Law. I took this class as an elective and was a little nervous about its difficulty going into it but as long as you care and are willing to put a little effort in, no one should have a problem getting a good grade. This class is at 8:30 and I have no problem getting up for it because its so interesting!

I think her classes are very chill and enjoyable. The content of this course is helpful beyond college life, and she's a nice professor, too. I'd recommend reading the material (she doesn't assign much) and trying to participate in class discussions. Fair grading with clear criteria, you will do well as long as you care.

If you can, take her class for Ideas that Change the World! It's almost completely discussion-based, so attendance and participation are critical. There are 3 graded reflection essays that are super manageable. Getting an A is easy if you just attend class.

analysis.name: Erin Baker
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Requires attendance and will call you out for being on your phone / laptop. Lectures have almost nothing to do with climate engineering and are structured like primary school class. Class is entirely a waste of time, this professor should not be teaching engineering at UMass. Guest speaker accomplished more in one lecture than she has in weeks.

ENGIN 113? More like HIPPIE 101. Learned more about sustainability than I did engineering.  No need to even take notes or pay attention in class.  The lab is what I looked forward to the most, even though you teach yourself how to use desigining software.  Creo 2.0 is a must have for this class, since you'll be learning straight from the book

In class she mostly does practice problems that you are put into groups for, as long as you vaguely understood the reading they are not super hard. There is Iclicker attendance so attendance is mandatory. Homework is light apart from the readings that are occasionally extremely long. Exams are not a lot of questions but are open note and open book.

Extremely boring class. Has nothing to do with learning mechanical engineering. Learn more about economics than anything. Pretty much learn what you could easily learn in one day over the entire semester. Lots of guest speakers. No midterm or final and the quizzes are open note.  Project required that's worked on all semester.
Class was mad easy once you get the hang of excel

analysis.name: Brokk Toggerson
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 130 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Grading is fuzzy. Nothing is clear cut, and doesn't clarify if you ask about it. He's rude when communicating. He's one of those professors that the subject makes so much sense to him that he can't understand why it doesn't make sense to you. Classwork is easy, but exams are difficult and near impossible.

Showing up and filling in a scantron was 30% of the final grade. Literally just put down your name, SID, and a few bubbled answers and sneak out.  He tries really hard and believes in the power of TBL but it sucks ass. HW, lab, AND test material are not covered in class because he takes all class to explain 1 problem. Gotta learn on your own.

I have always disliked physics. I was very confused the whole semester. This is the lowest level physics class offered (I believe) and was not that easy. It was a "teach yourself ahead of lecture" type class and team based learning. I hope they improve the class structure. Only did well because participation grade was contributed GREATLY.

Does not teach. Literally, you are expected to just read the book and there is no lecture whatsoever. Want to take physics with Toggerson? You're better off just buying a book and teaching yourself than paying $20k to have a professor tell you to read a book and teach yourself. Thanks UMass. I got to teach myself physics.

Brokk is nice. His teaching style, however, is harrowing. Studying for his violating 7 question exams is hell because he tries to reinvent the wheel with the way he teaches you normally stupidly easy physics concepts. The cherry on top is that I can't even find external resources to supplement literally anything Brokk taught in class. What a joke.

analysis.name: Jeffrey Beaulieu
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 260 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Two exams and a final. Weekly homework, graded tough. Go to class and discussion and get 6 points on your average. GO TO OFFICE HOURS!!! it's easy if you get help. He's really nice and tries to be clear. Discussion is optional but helpful. Tests are straightforward, offers review sessions that are extremely helpful! Not a bad class if you try hard!

I am a d1 Jeff glazer. I suck at math and this guy was so helpful when instructing as he focused on the why we do things instead of just doing it. As an extra plus, I love his voice he sounds like Obama. Homework is super easy but MyMathLab is expensive and sucks as a program. Jeff I love you I wish I could take you to Math 127 with me.

This is the only math class I have ever felt confident in. HIGHLY recommend Jeff.
All homework assignments will be on Pearson's My Math Lab. This will give you the opportunity to try problems multiple times and find study materials on what you struggle with. Each class is recorded and uploaded to Canvas in Echo360.

Jeff is such a nice guy and he explains most topics well. All exams are multiple choice and typically no more than 20 questions, 5 being free points! Attendance is not mandatory and lectures are recorded, but he looks sad if you don't come to class :( so go to class for king Jeff we love him

I've NEVER succeeded in math until having Jeff as a professor. He explains everything so simply, and his tests are beyond doable. Not to mention he's so kind and wholesome. As a sophomore, he's honestly my favorite professor thus far! I genuinely have nothing negative to say about him or his class.

analysis.name: Hendrik Van den Berg
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 70 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He failed half of the 200 person lecture on the first midterm. He goes off on tangents in lecture and isn't straightforward with information that we need to know for the exams, but instead likes to spew his extremely liberal idealogy that is irrelevant. He also assigns very long readings, but no powerpoints to help supplement the readings.

Compiles extremely fascinating content pertaining to the course. Knowledgeable about history, politics, and of course, econ and creation of economic models. Assignments are short and just check you pay attention in lecture and do some of the readings. Accessible outside of class and not hard to talk to. Could listen to his lectures for hours.

He's definitely a tough grader. He often goes on tangents about his time in Brazil and throws the class off course. About 20% of the things he says are actually important (and you'll miss them if you don't pay attention). Studied for 2 hours off of YouTube tutorials for his final and learned the same material that he took 6 weeks to explain.

The class is graded pretty much on 2 things only (the midterm and final). The exams are open notes (handwritten only) and they are open ended. If you can show that you did the readings, you'll do good. He is a very intelligent and kind person. He takes attendance and forbids any electronics. Overall, would take again.

All throughout the semester you thought you were going to do well because the TA graded the homework. He grades the exams himself and failed nearly everyone. Do not take any class with him. This is the most absurdly graded course I have taken in my 3 years of college.

analysis.name: Joseph Bergan
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I enjoyed how he used personal research experiences to explain different concepts in the class. Attendance is mandatory, and lectures can be slow at some points, but overall, they are very engaging. Didn't do well on the first two exams, but once I understood the format of the exams, I scored a lot better. Would recommend!
Reason I pursued neuroscience. If you have even a passing interest in neuroscience then you must take this class. If you believe the lectures are boring then you should ask questions, engage.

I really enjoyed this class! Prof Bergan is a super knowledgeable person with a lot of fascinating insights into neuroscience related things, so I'd encourage reaching out to him outside of class. Lectures are important, as his slides don't have everything written out. Grading on reaction papers is a little unclear though.

Bergan is knowledgable and understanding. If you are interested in neuroscience research, he is a good person to talk to. As a lecturer, he can be boring with no clickers or group work, making it hard to take notes and remain engaged. His slides don't really have words on them and there is so much content. But, exams and papers are relatively easy.

If you're interested in neuroscience, he is the professor to take. However, the class is lecture-heavy, and there is not a lot of room for participation. There is no homework, but there are weekly quizzes, four reaction papers to readings, and one final paper. For the exams, they are lecture-heavy which can sometimes be hard to pay attention for.

analysis.name: Kathleen Brown
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Kathleen runs one of the easiest classes you will take, but there were times when she gave blatantly false or outdated information, and if you have a background in the social sciences, you may notice how she butchers some topics and misses a lot of nuance. I'd love to take her class on American Indian Law, but 201H with her was unfulfilling.
Great professor. She provides all material needed for finishing reading. She is also very kind and caring plus nonchalant.

Kathleen is LITERALLY the BEST professor you will ever find at UMass!!!! She understands that this class isn't meant to dominate your life and so she keeps it fairly simple and gives clear instructions. And everyday at the end of class she offers you help and to go to her office with her. She also gives some extra credit assignments during semester

Class=mandatory, but get out early some days.  The books are interesting and as long as you're trying, shell help you. DO NOT fall asleep or be late.  BUY the MLA handbook and bring it to class. Shes very proud of being native american so dont her started on how the world has tortured her people. Thinks shes the greatest, but its funny so its okay.

I loved Kathleen's class. I strongly feel like an honors requirement like 291A should supplement the curriculum, not overtake your semester, and Kathleen did a great job with it. The reading was light; we had no essays and only one project. You don't have to buy the books; she provides the passages online. Would recommend her for this class.

analysis.name: Elizabeth Cook
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I love Beth!!! She's the best. If you're struggling, go to her. She cares so so much and really wants everyone to be heard, and supported in their learning. I credit so much of my love for epi to her because she makes it so interesting and fun. My fav UMass prof.
the professor is nice, quite lecture heavy but interesting topics - feels like sphhs150 but with some challenge
Worst class I've ever taken. Expectations are not clear at all and she randomly crashes out on the class. Do not take her class ever.

This is the second course I have taken with Dr. Cook; she is an amazing professor. Lectures are clear and she is very kind about any sort of questions, both she and the TAs are very accessible out of class. Attendance is mandatory but I missed half and still got an A. SUPER EASY CLASS!

Love her as a prof! Such a slay. Very fair and caring! (always asking and implementing feedback). Graded based on participation (to get a perfect score u had to come to 20 classes), 3 exams, weekly moodle quizzes, and homework. There is extra credit and u can make cheat sheets for exams. Not an "easy" A but veryyy attainable + worthwhile if you try

analysis.name: Cruz Davis
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: The entire semester, he did not reply to any email I ever sent him. Gets very frustrated when you don't understand the content and often refuses to further explain because we "should just know this by now". Is always 10+ minutes late to class. Understandable to think this will be an "Easy A" gen ed. IT. WILL. NOT.

He changed the entire curriculum from medical ethics to philosophy of medicine because he was too embarrassed to talk about abortion and pregnancy. He gives heavy lectures and grades each student very differently. He also changed student's grades on assignments at the very last minute before grades were due. DO NOT take his class, RUN.

He doesn't have slides for the class and doesn't post anything on canvas besides readings so you have to be in class. He does not follow his own syllabus and says he can change your grade by any percentage he feels based off your effort and participation. It doesn't feel like he knows what hes talking about half of the time. I would NEVER suggest!

Did not follow the syllabus, got rid of assignments and added others, HAVE TO ATTEND lecture or else you will get a bad grade, not helpful when you go to office hours and lowk creepy. Try to avoid this class unless you want uneccessary stress and anxiety every time you attend lecture. Changes grades up/down 5% as he sees fit!!!!

The professor is always late to class by at least 10 minutes and has no structure to the course. His assignments are vague, none of us know what we are supposed to do. He talks about helping you with assignments during office hours but gives close to no feedback and expects you to just do better. Would never recommend this prof

analysis.name: Chul Park
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I had to withdraw from this class. Mandatory attendance and 2% off your total grade every time you missed no matter what. There was no homework which was a pro and con. The con being the class was very exam heavy. The average on the first exam was a 50% and the second exam was the on the Monday right after Spring Break. Noted slides are not posted.

Professor Park does not upload his annotated notes online for review. No homework is assigned, which sounds nice, but exams are very heavily factored into your overall grade. Exams are also difficult, with the average on the first exam being 50. Attendance is also mandatory, and almost 2% of your grade is lost just for missing a class each time.

Not a lot of homework but lab assignments are long and difficult. He is lecture heavy and attendance isn't mandatory but he doesn't upload annotated notes. He is a poor lecturer and takes a long time to answer emails. He doesn't provide much to prepare for exams. Nice guy but bad professor. Don't expect much help or sympathy.

Overall layout of the class was very disorganized and confusing. We never finished the curriculum set out in the syllabus and topics covered were disproportionally covered on the exams. No HW so the exams are everything and Park gives little to no insight or guidance on how to prepare for exams. Non-responsive to emails. Grading is pretty lenient.

He likes to teach with an overhead projector with handouts/packets that you fill in as each lecture progresses. Lectures are late during the day on TuTh. Homeworks are relatively straightforward and simple if you put in some thoughts and the exams are a little more difficult but that is to be expected. He takes attendance so don't skip.

analysis.name: Garrett Cahill
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 71 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Took over the summer. Very clear criteria listed in the syllabus. It's easy to pick yourself back up after falling behind, but you should still stay on track. He may not always be clear and he may look for his opinion as the right answer but this forces you to really analyze material and think things through conceptually which is a beneficial skill in life in general. true comm majors would appreciate

analysis.name: Robert Feingold

Robert Feingold is hands down the best professor I've had through my college career. The topic itself is challenging, but he does a great job breaking down what you need to know. Robert is a great professor. His class is hard, but he does his best to help you succeed. analysis.name: Steve Fox

Aside from a few papers and books to read, the work is not too intense in this class, and he's always flexible about due dates and willing to cooperate with students. Class is heavy on discussions.
